title: UX Tutorial
subtitle: Orientation and step-by-step workflow walkthroughs
intro: Use this page when onboarding new staff or refreshing a specific process. Each walkthrough lists portal pages in a sensible order; open links in sequence or jump ahead. For full reference text, open Help or the printable manual in the repo.

orientation.title: App orientation (first session)
orientation.description: How the portal is organized: home, country, navigation, and where to find documentation.
steps: Start at the Dashboard for a snapshot of sites, counts, and health indicators.
Confirm the country (and portfolio, if shown) in the header matches the data you intend to work on — APIs and currency are per country.
Use the left sidebar for day-to-day work: Operations (reports, financials), Customer Data, Commerce, and System (tables, export, Help).
Open Help for the full operating manual with searchable sections.
Return here anytime via System → Tutorial for guided walkthroughs.

lifecycle.title: New customer lifecycle (happy path)
lifecycle.description: End-to-end flow from registration through metering; align with your local SOPs.
steps: Register the customer (identity, phone, site/concession, customer type).

When the customer pays the connection fee or full readyboard fee by mobile money, the portal recognises the exact amount automatically and queues it for finance verification — no manual matching required. If the customer can't pay it in full, grant an advance instead (see the Advances walkthrough).
Run the commissioning wizard to verify details, signatures, and paperwork.
Assign or confirm the meter and serial in the meter registry.
Use Customer Data for balances, charts, and transaction history (including any active advances).
Track funnel position on the Pipeline page.

payments.title: Payments & reconciliation
payments.description: When SMS gateway data is missing or needs review, and how to trace credits.
steps: Record a missed mobile-money payment so 1PDB and SparkMeter stay aligned. The classifier auto-detects connection / readyboard fees by exact amount; an active advance is repaid before electricity credit is applied.
Work the verification queue for fees and deposits that need human matching — connection and readyboard fees auto-route here.
Browse Transactions for recent credits and amounts site-wide; the kWh balance column reflects the kWh balance after each row.
Drill into Customer Data for a specific account’s history, balance context, and any Active Advances panel.

advances.title: Connection / readyboard advances
advances.description: Grant, manage, and reconcile fee advances when a customer can't pay the full amount up-front.
steps: Confirm the country fee amounts on the Tariffs page (Connection & Readyboard Fees card). These are the exact amounts the classifier looks for and the defaults pre-filled when granting an advance.

On the Advances page, click 'New advance', pick the customer, type (connection or readyboard), repayment fraction (default 50%), monthly fee % (premium), and upload the signed contract — the contract upload is required.

Once active, every electricity payment automatically siphons the repayment fraction toward the advance; the rest is credited as kWh. The ledger shows every grant, repayment, monthly fee accrual, contract replacement, and write-off.
Open Customer Data for the affected account to see the Active Advances panel with progress bar; deep-link back to the Advances page to edit or replace the contract.
Recognised fee payments still flow through Payment Verification — the advance is only repaid by subsequent electricity payments, not by the matched fee.

reporting.title: Operations & reporting
reporting.description: Quarterly O&M, financial analytics, meter checks, and maintenance context.
steps: O&M Report: quarterly customer, consumption, and sales analytics.
Financial: ARPU trends, revenue by site, portfolio views.
Check Meters: compare primary vs check-meter signals where deployed.
Maintenance Log: ticket context linked to field work.

commerce.title: Tariffs, financing, and exports
commerce.description: Rate configuration, asset financing, and bulk extracts for analysis.
steps: Tariffs: site/concession rates that drive kWh conversion for new payments.
Financing: products, agreements, and ledger views.
Export: CSV/XLSX extracts for offline analysis (respect data-handling policy).

dataAudit.title: Data browsers, audit, and integrations
dataAudit.description: Raw views, change history, and uGridPlan sync status.
steps: Accounts: browse account rows and filters.
Tables: SQL table browser for advanced inspection.
Mutations: audit trail with revert where supported.
Sync: uGridPlan integration status and recent operations.

safetyOverride.title: Meter safety override — emergency relay cutoff
safetyOverride.description: How to force a meter's relay open in an emergency, and how to restore normal billing afterward.
steps: Open the Meter Registry and locate the target meter. The Safety column shows a green auto badge (normal) or red OVERRIDE badge (cutoff active).
Click the safety badge to open the two-step confirmation dialog. Select off (safety cutoff) and enter a mandatory reason — this is recorded in the audit log. Optional notes are saved alongside.
Click Confirm, then Confirm again within the 3-second countdown — this double-click prevents accidental cutoffs that would deprive a customer of paid-for electricity.

To restore normal billing, reopen the dialog and toggle back to auto. The relay will not immediately re-close; reconnection is handled by the normal billing cycle or the customer's next mobile-money top-up.

smsLog.title: SMS outbound log & delivery audit
smsLog.description: Inspect every outbound SMS and verify whether the carrier actually delivered it.
steps: Open the SMS Log under System → SMS Log. Every outbound SMS is automatically recorded — type, phone, account, message body, and two delivery statuses.
Filter by SMS type (balance / welcome / contract), success/failure, phone, account, or date range to narrow down what you need.

The Gateway column tells you whether the SMS host accepted the request (green Queued = success; red Failed = error). The CM.com column is the carrier delivery confirmation — Accepted means it reached the handset; Rejected or another status means the carrier refused or could not deliver it.
Click any row to expand the full message body, raw gateway URL, CM.com status timestamp and error code, and any internal error message.
